stories.title: Thunder 131-108 Lakers Game 3 — Ajay Mitchell's Historic Line Pushes OKC to 3-0 Series Lead

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
#ThunderUp are 7-0 in the playoffs after routing the Lakers 131-108.

Ajay Mitchell: 24 pts, 10 ast, 3 stl, ZERO turnovers.

That line puts him alongside Magic Johnson, Chris Paul, and Haliburton.

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
The Lakers led by 2 at halftime (11 three-pointers!) then OKC outscored them by 25 in the second half.

SGA 23/9. Holmgren 18/9. Mitchell 24/10/3stl.

This Thunder team is built different. #ThunderUp

facebook.long_form:
Oklahoma City came into Los Angeles and did exactly what great teams do — they let the Lakers believe for a half, then took over and destroyed them.

In Game 3 on Saturday night, the Thunder beat the Lakers 131-108 to take a 3-0 series lead. Oklahoma City is now 7-0 in the 2026 playoffs and looking every bit like a championship team.

The story of the night was Ajay Mitchell. The 22-year-old guard posted 24 points, 10 assists, 3 steals, and zero turnovers while shooting 10-of-17. According to ESPN, that combination makes him just the fourth player since 1990 to record 20+ points, 10+ assists, 3+ steals, and zero turnovers in a playoff game — joining Magic Johnson, Chris Paul, and Tyrese Haliburton.

Here's the wild part: the Lakers actually led at halftime. They hit 11 three-pointers in the first two quarters and held a 2-point edge. Then Oklahoma City — SGA (23 pts, 9 ast), Holmgren (18 pts, 9-14 FG), Wallace (16 pts) — turned it on and outscored Los Angeles by 25 in the second half.

LeBron James finished 7-of-19 from the field for 19 points. Hachimura led the Lakers with 21. Reaves had 17 with 9 assists. It wasn't enough. Without Luka Doncic, who remains uncleared for contact, LA simply doesn't have the firepower to sustain a run against this Thunder team.
facebook.image_caption: Oklahoma City 7-0. Ajay Mitchell's 24/10/3stl/0TO joins Magic, CP3, and Haliburton in the record books. The Thunder are rolling. #ThunderUp

article.headline: Ajay Mitchell's Historic Line Fuels Thunder's 131-108 Rout of Lakers in Game 3

stories.title: Cavaliers 116-109 Pistons Game 3 — Mitchell's 35 Pts and 2,000 Career Postseason Points Cut Deficit to 2-1

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Donovan Mitchell: 35 pts, AND 2,000 career playoff points in 73 games.

Tied 3rd-fastest among active players. 9th in NBA history.

Cleveland stays alive. #Cavs 116, #Pistons 109.

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Harden clutch. Mitchell 35. Strus contributing off the bench.

Cleveland gutted out a must-win at home and cut the deficit to 2-1.

This series isn't over. #Cavs

facebook.long_form:
Cleveland's season isn't over — and Donovan Mitchell made absolutely sure of that on Saturday night.

Mitchell erupted for 35 points as the Cavaliers beat the Detroit Pistons 116-109 in Game 3 at Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse, cutting the series deficit to 2-1. Detroit still leads, but the Cavs got a crucial home win to avoid falling into an almost certain elimination hole.

The milestone: Mitchell reached 2,000 career postseason points during this game, achieving the mark in his 73rd playoff appearance. That places him tied for third-fastest among active players to hit 2,000 postseason points and ninth in NBA history overall. It's another data point in an already-remarkable postseason career.

After collapsing in the clutch in Games 1 and 2 on the road, Cleveland found its late-game execution. James Harden delivered critical baskets in the final three minutes when Detroit threatened to take control. Max Strus provided energy off the bench.

The series shifts back to Detroit for Game 4, where Cade Cunningham and the Pistons will try to push their lead to 3-1.
facebook.image_caption: 35 points. 2,000 career playoff points in 73 games. Tied 3rd-fastest among active players. Donovan Mitchell delivered when Cleveland needed him most. #Cavs

stories.title: Knicks-76ers Game 4 Today — Can New York Complete the Sweep?

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
History is on the line TODAY at 3:30 PM ET on ABC.

No NBA team has EVER come back from 0-3 to win a series.

The Knicks are one win from their first best-of-7 sweep since 1999. #NewYorkForever

facebook.long_form:
NBA history is literally at stake this afternoon in Philadelphia.

The New York Knicks carry a 3-0 series lead into Game 4 of the Eastern Conference Semifinals against the 76ers, with tip-off at 3:30 PM ET on ABC at Wells Fargo Center. No team in NBA history has ever come back from an 0-3 series deficit to win — and this Knicks team shows no signs of backing down.

In Game 3, New York was without OG Anunoby (hamstring) and still won by 14. Brunson had 33 and 9. Bridges shot 8-of-14 for 23. KAT posted 8/12/7. Hart did Hart things (12/11). Shamet came off the bench for 15 in 26 minutes and finished a +20. Joel Embiid returned for Philly and scored 18 in 35 minutes — not enough.

Philadelphia's hope today rests on Embiid (questionable again) staying healthy and on Tyrese Maxey and Paul George having career-defining performances in an elimination game. The Knicks are chasing their first best-of-seven sweep since their 1999 Finals run.

Watch today at 3:30 PM ET on ABC.
facebook.image_caption: No team has ever come back from 0-3. The Knicks are one win from their first playoff sweep since 1999. History on the line TODAY at 3:30 PM ET on ABC. #NewYorkForever

stories.title: Spurs-Timberwolves Game 4 Tonight — Wemby vs. Edwards for Series Control

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Wemby (39/15/5 blk in G3) vs. Edwards (32/14/6) — Game 4 TONIGHT at 7:30 PM ET on NBC.

Spurs lead 2-1. A win puts SA one step from the Conference Finals. #GoSpursGo

facebook.long_form:
If you want to watch the future of NBA basketball play out in real time, tune into Game 4 tonight.

Victor Wembanyama and Anthony Edwards square off again at 7:30 PM ET on NBC at Target Center in Minneapolis, with the San Antonio Spurs holding a 2-1 series lead. A Spurs win puts them up 3-1 and within striking distance of the Western Conference Finals. A Wolves win ties the series at 2-2 and completely resets the stakes.

Game 3 in Minnesota was spectacular. Wembanyama scored 39 on 13-of-18 shooting, grabbed 15 rebounds, and blocked 5 shots — 18 of those points came in the fourth quarter. Stephon Castle added 13 and 12 assists in the 115-108 Spurs win.

Edwards countered with 32 points, 14 rebounds, and 6 assists — a ridiculous line that wasn't enough. Back on his home floor tonight, expect Ant to come out swinging.

San Antonio is favored by 4.5 points. But when these two are involved, the spread means nothing.
facebook.image_caption: Wembanyama 39/15/5 blk. Edwards 32/14/6. Game 4 is TONIGHT at 7:30 PM ET on NBC. Spurs lead 2-1. Who makes their move? #GoSpursGo #Timberwolves

stories.title: Lakers Facing Historic Sweep — Can LeBron Force Game 4 or Does OKC Close?

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
The #LakeShow trail 0-3 after last night's 131-108 loss.

No NBA team has ever come back from 0-3 in 150+ chances. LeBron is 41. Luka isn't cleared.

The math is brutal. #ThunderUp

facebook.long_form:
This may be the most daunting challenge of LeBron James's postseason career.

After Saturday night's 131-108 loss to the Oklahoma City Thunder, the Los Angeles Lakers are 0-3 in the series. No team in NBA history has ever come back from an 0-3 deficit — not in 150-plus attempts. Oklahoma City is 7-0 in the 2026 playoffs and playing arguably the best basketball of any team this postseason.

LeBron shot 7-of-19 for 19 points in Game 3. Rui Hachimura led the Lakers with 21. Austin Reaves had 17 with 9 assists. The Lakers actually led at halftime after hitting 11 three-pointers — but OKC outscored them by 25 after the break.

Luka Doncic, recovering from a Grade 2 hamstring strain, is still not cleared for contact. Without their second star, the Lakers lack the creation needed to compete against a Thunder team that has five guys who can take over a game.

The only question left is whether LeBron and the Lakers can fight hard enough to force Oklahoma City to really earn it — or whether this ends quickly.
facebook.image_caption: Down 0-3. LeBron shot 7-of-19. Luka isn't cleared. And they face a Thunder team that is 7-0 in these playoffs. The odds for the #LakeShow have never been longer. #ThunderUp

article.headline: Lakers Staring Down History: What an 0-3 Deficit Means for LeBron and LA's Season
